pages.raw_text:
10y United Press LeasedN Wire)


CHEMAWA, Ore, Feb. 9.-Ac-
cording to reports received by let
ter at Chemawa from Indians on
the Warm Springs reservation, the
Indians there are greatly excited
over the newspaper reports that as
a result of the Hill and Hartman
railroads passing through their
reservations their surplus lands are
to be given to the white men, and
that 10000 white families will soon
be living on their lands.


A council was called and three
of their tribesmen were elected to
go to Washington, d. c., and talk
with "the Great Father" Albert
Kickup, Henry Ineapahnah and
Ben Wilson were appointed to
make the trip. They were in
structed to tell "the Great Father"
that they had not been paid yet for


land already taken from them.
